items.title: As vaccination rates fall in Australia, a grieving British mother has a warning

children.content: Measles killed Rebecca Archer's daughter Renae slowly and silently over a decade. She has a message for the growing number of parents hesitant to have their children vaccinated.

gallery.altText: A woman with a serious expression holds a picture of a young girl who is smiling.

items.title: Will Australia be taken for a ride in the AI and data centre boom?

children.content: More than $150 billion is lined up for data centre development and investment in Australia, but there are questions as to whether the boom will provide benefits beyond a short-term construction boost.

image.altText: A data centre

items.title: 'Rain bomb', mini tornadoes: Debunking Australia's greatest weather myths

children.content: Meteorologic rhetoric in Australia, including from the media, can at times be inaccurate, misconstrued or just completely fictitious.

image.altText: A graphic showing three weather myth cards behind a stormy outback: one on rain, one on mini tornadoes, one on 'rain bombs'.

items.title: How looksmaxxing trend is affecting young men

children.content: While it's a bit of fun for some, the social media trend known as looksmaxxing can have major impacts on young men's physical and mental health.

items.title: Diver dies after being bitten by 4.5m shark off Albany, WA

items.title: 'Everything looked real': How scammers are hijacking your holiday

items.title: Russian oil facilities hit by second Ukrainian drone attack after Putin rejects talks

items.title: What's driving Australia's lentil boom?

children.content: Australia's winter crop production is expected to be down this year, but lentils are bucking the trend and headed towards a record.

items.title: How Everest guide survived six days alone with no oxygen and a few chocolates

items.title: A Sydney mother died after drinking 67 cups of water under 24-hour watch

items.title: Wall Street suffers worst hit of 2026 so far amid massive stock sell-off

children.content: The US stock market has experienced its worst losses in months due to large sell-offs of shares in technology giants and fears of a looming rate hike in the wake of a strong jobs figures report.

items.title: Fastest-rising women's cancer in Australia still has a low profile

children.content: The incidence of uterine cancer has doubled over the past 25 years. But it's also a disease without a public profile, which means many women aren't aware of the warning signs or the risk factors.

items.title: Andrew made money subletting cottages on his almost rent-free estate, watchdog finds

children.content: Former prince Andrew pocketed income from cottages on the Windsor estate while living at Royal Lodge under a lease that required virtually no rent, a watchdog report has found.

items.title: New AUKUS risk as Trump orders 'Golden Fleet' battleships

items.title: The US ally getting nuclear-powered submarines with no AUKUS deal

children.content: South Korea is taking a different path to Australia in building nuclear-powered submarines, but its plan still hinges on the United States.

items.title: Patients 'chained' at Sri Lankan nursing home where 13 died in fire

children.content: A "chained" patient was among more than a dozen people who died during a fire at an unregistered nursing home in Sri Lanka, according to a staff member.

items.title: US and Iran trade fire in Strait of Hormuz

children.content: The US military says it has shot down four drones Iran has launched toward the Strait of Hormuz and struck some of Tehran's coastal surveillance radar sites in response.
